questions.question: Syntax is defined in Lesson 3 as what?

choices.B: The form or structure of expressions, statements, and program units

questions.question: Semantics is defined as what?

choices.A: The set of sentences in a language
choices.C: The meaning of expressions, statements, and program units
choices.D: The set of terminals and nonterminals

questions.question: What do syntax and semantics together provide?
Which group is listed as users of a language definition?
A sentence is defined as what?

choices.D: A string of characters over some alphabet

questions.question: A language is defined as what?
A lexeme is defined as what?

choices.D: The lowest level syntactic unit of a language

questions.question: Which are examples of lexemes given in the lesson?
A token is defined as what?

choices.D: A sentence with only terminal symbols

questions.question: In formal definitions of languages, what is a recognizer?

choices.C: A device that reads input strings and decides if they belong to the language
choices.D: A list of tokens and lexemes

questions.question: What is an example of a recognizer?

choices.A: Syntax analysis part of a compiler

questions.question: What is a generator in formal language definition?

choices.D: A device that generates sentences of a language

questions.question: Context-free grammars were developed by whom and when?
What were context-free grammars originally meant to describe?
What class of languages do context-free grammars define?
Backus-Naur Form (BNF) was invented by whom to describe the syntax of Algol 58?
When was BNF invented?
What is the relationship between BNF and context-free grammars?

choices.C: BNF is equivalent to context-free grammars

questions.question: In BNF, what are abstractions also called?
In BNF, terminals are what?
In BNF, a rule has a left-hand side (LHS) and a right-hand side (RHS). The LHS is what?
The RHS of a BNF rule is what?

choices.C: A string of terminals and/or nonterminals

questions.question: How are nonterminals often enclosed in BNF?
What is a grammar?

choices.D: A finite non-empty set of rules

questions.question: What is a start symbol in a grammar?

choices.C: A special element of the nonterminals of a grammar

questions.question: How are syntactic lists described in BNF?
What is a derivation?

choices.A: A repeated application of rules starting with start symbol and ending with a sentence

questions.question: What is a sentential form?

choices.D: Every string of symbols in a derivation

questions.question: In a derivation, a sentence is a sentential form that has only what?
A leftmost derivation is one in which which nonterminal is expanded each time?
Can a derivation be neither leftmost nor rightmost?

choices.A: No, it must be one or the other

questions.question: What is a parse tree?

choices.C: A hierarchical representation of a derivation

questions.question: A grammar is ambiguous if and only if what?

choices.A: It has exactly one parse tree
choices.B: It generates a sentential form that has two or more distinct parse trees

questions.question: In EBNF, optional parts are placed in which symbols?
In EBNF, how are alternative parts of RHSs notated?

choices.D: Placed inside parentheses and separated via vertical bars

questions.question: In EBNF, repetitions (0 or more) are placed in which symbols?
What does EBNF make more compact compared to BNF?
In recent variations of EBNF, what is used instead of =>?
Static semantics has nothing to do with what?
Can context-free grammars describe all of the syntax of programming languages?
Which is an example of a context-free but cumbersome construct?
Which is an example of a non-context-free constraint listed in Lesson 3?

choices.B: Variables must be declared before they are used

questions.question: Why do programmers need a methodology and notation for semantics?
What would be possible with a good semantics notation according to the lesson?
Operational semantics describes meaning by doing what?

choices.A: Executing statements on a machine and observing state changes

questions.question: In operational semantics, what defines the meaning of a statement?

choices.C: The change in the state of the machine (memory, registers, etc.)

questions.question: What is needed to use operational semantics for a high-level language?
Why is a hardware pure interpreter too expensive for operational semantics?

choices.D: The lesson states it's too expensive but doesn't specify details

questions.question: What problem exists with software pure interpreters for operational semantics?

choices.A: Detailed characteristics of particular computer make actions difficult to understand

questions.question: A better alternative for operational semantics involves what two components?

choices.C: A translator and a simulator for an idealized computer

questions.question: When is operational semantics good according to the lesson?
VDL was used for describing semantics of which language?
Denotational semantics is based on what?
How is denotational semantics characterized?

choices.A: The most abstract semantics description method

questions.question: Who originally developed denotational semantics?
In denotational semantics, what must be defined for each language entity?
How are the meanings of language constructs defined in denotational semantics?

choices.C: By only the values of the program's variables

questions.question: In denotational semantics, loops are converted from what to what?
Why is recursion preferred over iteration in denotational semantics?

choices.B: It's easier to describe with mathematical rigor

questions.question: Can denotational semantics be used to prove correctness of programs?
What is a drawback of denotational semantics?

choices.C: Because of its complexity, it's of little use to language users
choices.D: It doesn't work for modern languages

questions.question: Axiomatic semantics is based on what?
What was the original purpose of axiomatic semantics?
In axiomatic semantics, what are the logic expressions called?
What is a precondition?
What is a postcondition?
What is a weakest precondition?

choices.A: The least restrictive precondition that will guarantee the postcondition

questions.question: In the program proof process, what is the postcondition for the entire program?
How do you verify a program is correct using axiomatic semantics?

choices.C: Work back through program; if precondition on first statement matches specification, program is correct

questions.question: How are state changes defined in operational semantics?
How are state changes defined in denotational semantics?
What are the three primary methods of semantics description mentioned in the summary?
BNF and context-free grammars are described as what?
What can an attribute grammar describe?

choices.B: Both syntax and semantics of a language
